ASSAY component gallery

Live components — the demonstrator's actual code, rendered over Meridian vignette fixture data (SPEC-04). Components, not product: no service, no compute, no plan exists behind this page. Every assessed value is banded with provenance welded on (ASSAY-DEC-9); the Meridian scenario is engineered fiction (ASSAY-DEC-8).

The knowledge base, K1–K14

Minimal S1 knowledge table (SPEC-05)

The knowledge service's four discipline moments, rendered by the actual firewall over the same fixtures: the refused K10 save (banner), the contested K12 pair (compile-blocking flag), K8's waiver and single-source markings, and confidence-lint cautions. Banded honesty holds — no bare assessed scalar appears (G2).

Refused · encoding_violation offending: K10
K10: an assumption may never be a hard constraint — no waiver can license one.
Save K10 (an assumption dressed as a hard constraint): the system declines laundered judgement.

Compile — knowledge → CompiledWorld (SPEC-06)

The Stage-2 demo moment, run by the actual compile service. Contested never compiles (G5): with the K12 mine-stock pair disputed, the compile refuses rather than average an irreconcilable band into a channel —

Refused · contested_knowledge offending: K12a, K12b
K12a, K12b: contested knowledge never compiles — resolve the dispute first.

Resolve the contest, and the recompile builds the world. Channels are stored sparse — a per-channel default plus named region overrides (the MCOO idiom; no dense 1.2M-cell surface is ever stored or hashed). Every channel value walks back to the named knowledge it came from, with its owner (G3). stamp 4fa981bd2093ed28… · 9 knowledge objects consumed · sparse channels (no dense cell stored)

regionvaluefrom knowledgeprovenance
mobility · default 01 transit factor2
approach 3.757.5 m controlling depth11.25 K1 observed · highowner: CTF-M hydrographic cell
causeway 1020–40 tonnes50 K2 assessed · moderateassessment, not factowner: J-2 imagery cell
tide · default -10 m surge1 — no overrides (channel at default)
storm · default -10 m surge1
open_water 0.75000000000000011.1–1.8 m surge2.15 K9 steps 8–36reported · highassessment, not factowner: METOC
civil_density · default -10 persons1
port_district 2500035000–55000 persons65000 K3 reported · moderateassessment, not factowner: J-2 civil affairs
sensor · default -10 coverage index1
battery 4.510–21 days to repair26.5 K8 assessed · moderateassessment, not factsingle-sourceowner: J-2
threat · default -10 threat index1

Score & organise — the honest matrix (SPEC-07 · SPEC-08)

The Stage-3 demo moment, run end-to-end by the actual machinery: /plan/handful (SPEC-08) fans out over Meridian's four axes — approach, suppression, causeway, extraction — scores every candidate with the SPEC-07 scorer, and organises by banded non-domination into the genuinely distinct plans below. No author picked the set: a plan is kept only when no other conservatively beats it on every commitment (overlapping bands are honestly incomparable and both survive), and dropped only when strictly beaten everywhere — never on a scalar total (DEC-19). Each plan's reason is derived from the organiser, not captioned:

  • P-SwSoCcXe sweep-first · stand-off · contest · pull-early
    sweep-first · stand-off · contest · pull-early — leads the handful on C1, C3; non-dominated (no plan beats it on every commitment).
  • P-SeFfCcXe strait-early · fires-forward · contest · pull-early
    strait-early · fires-forward · contest · pull-early — leads the handful on C2, C4; non-dominated (no plan beats it on every commitment).
  • P-SeSoCcXe strait-early · stand-off · contest · pull-early
    strait-early · stand-off · contest · pull-early — leads the handful on C2, C3; non-dominated (no plan beats it on every commitment).
  • P-SwFfCcXe sweep-first · fires-forward · contest · pull-early
    sweep-first · fires-forward · contest · pull-early — leads the handful on C1, C4; non-dominated (no plan beats it on every commitment).

The same handful, as the S2 planner matrix. Verdicts are a four-stop scale — robust / marginal / tight / violated, one colour language, no decimals anywhere; the banded margin rides on hover, never a headline number (G2). Each verdict propagates its metric by interval arithmetic — worst-and-best-case on pure bands, no midpoint (DEC-15) — validated by the vignette §9 oracle cases (O-1–O-3 exact, O-4 containment). Pick a verdict, walk it to the assessment and owner it rests on: every cell was written with a scored_from edge back to the world, whose channels trace to named knowledge above. handful stamp 99ca45289c4582da… · 4 genuinely distinct plans, organised not authored · same stamp + seed ⇒ identical handful (G1) · every verdict carries a scored_from edge to the world (G3)

plan \ commitment C1C2C3C4C5C6
P-SwSoCcXe · sweep-first · stand-off · contest · pull-early robust robust marginal tight marginal robust
P-SeFfCcXe · strait-early · fires-forward · contest · pull-early robust robust violated robust marginal robust
P-SeSoCcXe · strait-early · stand-off · contest · pull-early robust robust marginal tight marginal robust
P-SwFfCcXe · sweep-first · fires-forward · contest · pull-early robust robust violated robust marginal robust

Relax — least-worst, never silence (SPEC-09)

The Stage-4 demo moment, run end-to-end by the actual machinery. Against the R3m world (both approaches mined, the causeway dropped) no plan satisfies C2, C3 and C4 together — by construction (vignette §6). /relax does not pick one compromise and hide the trade: it returns every inclusion-minimal way forward, each naming what it gives up in command language. The sacrifices are computed — a commitment is sacrificed only when the SPEC-07 scorer returns violated for it — not authored. Ranked least-worst first by the commander's ordinal tiers (must / should / prefer, no numeric weight — DEC-19): the two should-sacrifices lead, the must-sacrifice (C2, "the strait opens two days late") is ranked last but present — never dropped, never silent (G4). Plans that give up more — {C2,C4}, {C3,C4} — are inclusion-dominated and never shown as separate options. relax stamp 69fd05662fc5b616… · 3 least-worst candidates over R3m, computed not authored · never empty, never a silent drop (G4) · tie-break stated (DEC-19) · each candidate cited_in the report back to named knowledge (G3)

  • least-worst #1 RX-fires C3 should
    Fires forward — suppress the FAC berths so the group crosses clean and on time, but the fires fall into the populated harbourfront where the craft berth among the fishing fleet.
    • gives up: No fires into the populated port district (C3)
  • least-worst #2 RX-parallel C4 should
    Parallel sweep — both approaches cleared at once, fastest to open the strait, but the amphibious group crosses the mined strait before the battery and FACs are suppressed.
    • gives up: Amphibious group not exposed to unsuppressed battery or FAC threat (C4)
  • least-worst #3 RX-sequential C2 must
    Sequential sweep — clear one approach then the other, keeping the group clear and the harbourfront quiet, but the strait opens at D+9, two days late.
    • gives up: Halcyon Strait swept and open to deep-draught traffic by D+7 (C2)

Tie-break — Same-tier sacrifices were ordered by commitment id (C3 vs C4) — a stated, commander-owned placeholder for within-tier priority, not a claim that one sacrifice outranks the other (ASSAY-DEC-19: no numeric weight; ties stated, never silent).

Generated from fixtures/ by npm run gallery · identifiers frozen per assay-vignette.md §8 · compile per research note docs/research/02-compile.md · score per docs/research/03-score-plan.md · relax per docs/research/04-relaxation.md
